---
title: "DPmixGPD"
---

::: {.section}
::: {.container}

# DPmixGPD

Bayesian semiparametric modeling for heavy-tailed outcomes using Dirichlet-process mixtures with optional GPD tails, with conditional and causal extensions.

[Getting Started](getting-started.html){.btn .btn-primary} [Decision Guide](decision-guide.html){.btn .btn-outline-primary} [Reference (pkgdown)](pkgdown/reference/index.html){.btn .btn-outline-primary}

<div style="margin-top:.75rem;font-size:.95rem;color:rgba(0,0,0,.7)">
Explore the curated documentation in <a href="reference.html">Docs</a> or jump to <a href="faq.html">FAQ</a>.
</div>

:::
:::


::: {.section}
::: {.container}

## Choose your path

::: {.grid-3}

::: {.cardx}
### New users
Start with a minimal workflow, then use the Decision Guide to select a backend + kernel.

[Getting Started](getting-started.html){.btn .btn-primary} [Decision Guide](decision-guide.html){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
:::

::: {.cardx}
### Applied users
Go straight to the Docs hub, pick kernels, and use the Cookbook for templates. For exact arguments, use pkgdown.

[Docs](reference.html){.btn .btn-primary} [Kernels](kernels.html){.btn .btn-outline-primary} [Cookbook](cookbook.html){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
:::

::: {.cardx}
### Developers
Prefer the vanilla docs and function reference, or dig into build and contribution notes.

[Developers](developers.html){.btn .btn-primary} [Vanilla site](pkgdown/index.html){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
:::

:::

:::
:::


::: {.section}
::: {.container}

## Docs at a glance

::: {.grid-3}

::: {.cardx}
### Docs hub
Curated entry point to everything Quarto-side.

[Open Docs](reference.html){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
:::

::: {.cardx}
### Kernels
Distribution families, conventions, and practical guidance.

[Browse Kernels](kernels.html){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
:::

::: {.cardx}
### Cookbook
Workflow templates and common analysis patterns.

[Open Cookbook](cookbook.html){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
:::

::: {.cardx}
### Start here
Minimal setup and your first end-to-end run.

[Getting Started](getting-started.html){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
:::

::: {.cardx}
### FAQs
Short answers to common questions and gotchas.

[Open FAQ](faq.html){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
:::

::: {.cardx}
### Citation
How to cite the package and related work.

[How to cite](cite.html){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
:::

:::

:::
:::


::: {.section}
::: {.container}

## Latest update

::: {.cardx}
Documentation is now split cleanly: Quarto provides the curated front door, while pkgdown remains the authoritative vanilla API reference for developers.

[Read the update](news.html){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
:::

:::
:::


::: {.section}
::: {.container}

<hr>

<div style="display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:1rem;justify-content:space-between;align-items:center;font-size:.9rem;color:rgba(0,0,0,.65)">

<div>
© DPmixGPD · Bayesian semiparametric modeling for heavy-tailed data
</div>

<div>
<a href="cite.html">Cite</a> ·
<a href="pkgdown/index.html">Vanilla docs</a> ·
<a href="pkgdown/reference/index.html">Reference</a> ·
<a href="https://github.com/arnabaich96/DPmixGPD">GitHub</a>
</div>

</div>
